mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based economic expansion, which amplifies corporate profit growth and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO trends upward for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the CPIAUCSL threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized combined with rising residential investment indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over any 4-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reduces labor market slack, which lowers wage-push inflation risks and supports steady equity valuation expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening construction activity and consumer confidence, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5 percent in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST falls by the specified threshold.
